Package org.eventb.core.ast.tests

Class TestFormulaFactory

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.core.ast.tests.AbstractTests
org.eventb.core.ast.tests.TestFormulaFactory
public class TestFormulaFactory extends AbstractTests
Unit tests for factory methods which are not fully tested elsewhere.

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    private static final class 
    TestFormulaFactory.UnknownExtension
    Instances of this class must never be used to construct a formula factory.

    validIdentifierName()
    Ensures that method isValidIdentifierName() takes into account the version of the mathematical language supported by the formula factory instance.

  • Method Details

    • getTagForUnknownExtension

      public void getTagForUnknownExtension()
      Ensures that getTag() returns NO_TAG when passed an unknown extension.

    • getExtensionForSupportedTag

      public void getExtensionForSupportedTag()
      Ensures that getExtension() returns the extension when supported.

    • getExtensionForRegularTag

      public void getExtensionForRegularTag()
      Ensures that getExtension() returns null when given the tag of regular operator (not a formula extension).

    • getExtensionForUnsupportedTag

      public void getExtensionForUnsupportedTag()
      Ensures that getTag() returns null when passed an extension which is not supported by the factory.

    • hasExtensionForSupportedExtension

      public void hasExtensionForSupportedExtension()
      Ensures that hasExtension() returns true for a supported extension.

    • hasExtensionForUnknownExtension

      public void hasExtensionForUnknownExtension()
      Ensures that hasExtension() returns false for an unknown extension.

    • hasExtensionForUnsupportedExtension

      public void hasExtensionForUnsupportedExtension()
      Ensures that hasExtension() returns false for an unsupported extension.

    • assertArrayProtected

      private static final void assertArrayProtected(Object obj, Object[] array)
      Verifies that object construction is protected against array mutation. The test consists in verifying that the behavior of toString() is not impacted by changing the array.
      Parameters:
      obj - an object which was constructed with the given array
      array - a non-empty array
